code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Predicate-to-phrase mapping with natural language phrasing
    verb_map = {
        "almaMater": "studied at",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was originally named",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired on",
        "occupation": "worked as",
        "status": "is",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "flew on",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "was a citizen of",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as Chief of the Astronaut Office in",
        "title": "held the title",
        "ribbonAward": "received the",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by",
        "crewMembers": "included crew member",
        "representative": "was represented by",
        "alternativeName": "was also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senator",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    # Relative pronouns for connecting clauses
    relative_map = {
        "birthPlace": "which is in",
        "birthDate": "who was born on",
        "deathDate": "who died on",
        "deathPlace": "who died in",
        "nationality": "who was a citizen of",
        "occupation": "who worked as",
        "almaMater": "who studied at",
        "mission": "who flew on",
        "timeInSpace": "who spent",
        "alternativeName": "who was also known as",
        "awards": "who received",
        "status": "who is",
    }

    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    # Build sentence with proper clause connections
    main_subject = None
    main_clauses = []
    relative_clauses = []

    for subject, ts in grouped.items():
        if main_subject is None:
            main_subject = subject
            # Build main subject's clauses
            for t in ts:
                verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, f"has {t.predicate}")
                obj = t.object.strip('"')

                # Special handling for predicates
                if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
                    obj = f"{obj} minutes in space"
                elif t.predicate == "awards":
                    if obj.isdigit():
                        obj = f"{obj} awards"
                elif t.predicate == "status":
                    obj = obj.lower()
                elif t.predicate == "ribbonAward":
                    obj = obj.replace('.svg', '')

                main_clauses.append(f"{verb} {obj}")
        else:
            # Build relative clauses for additional subjects
            for t in ts:
                verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, f"has {t.predicate}")
                obj = t.object.strip('"')

                if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
                    obj = f"{obj} minutes in space"
                elif t.predicate == "awards":
                    if obj.isdigit():
                        obj = f"{obj} awards"
                elif t.predicate == "status":
                    obj = obj.lower()

                relative_clauses.append(f"{subject} {verb} {obj}")

    # Construct the final sentence
    if main_clauses:
        if len(main_clauses) == 1:
            sentence = f"{main_subject} {main_clauses[0]}"
        else:
            sentence = f"{main_subject} {main_clauses[0]}, " + ", ".join(main_clauses[1:-1]) + f", and {main_clauses[-1]}"

        # Add relative clauses if any
        if relative_clauses:
            sentence += ". " + ". ".join(relative_clauses) + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(relative_clauses) if relative_clauses else ""

    # Capitalize first character
    if sentence:
        return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    return ""

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
